findings.summary: PosGroup constants live in the query package but are imported by config and tagging layers, reversing the expected dependency direction
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup and calls PosGroup.ADJ etc. in collocatePosGroup()
SimpleTagger imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup and uses it in TaggedToken.getPosGroup()
PosGroup is a domain-level constant (4 string values) that does not belong to the query layer
findings.suggestion: Move PosGroup to a shared domain or constants package (e.g., pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.domain or .common) so that config, tagging, and query all depend on it without any package depending on query for a constant.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer's primary constructor directly instantiates BlackLabQueryExecutor, bypassing the QueryExecutor abstraction it also accepts
evidence: Line 71: public SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) throws IOException { this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); }
Line 76: A second constructor takes QueryExecutor (for testing), showing the abstraction exists but the production path bypasses it
The ownsExecutor flag is a code smell indicating lifecycle management is being manually layered on top of an inconsistent creation pattern
findings.suggestion: Remove the String-path constructor or make it a static factory; let callers always inject a QueryExecutor. If lifecycle ownership is needed, provide an explicit factory method SemanticFieldExplorer.forIndex(String) that documents the ownership contract.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer imports five inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer, tightly coupling the API layer to exploration implementation internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective
All five are accessed by field name (e.g., dn.noun, dn.sharedCount, ca.adjective) in the API handlers, encoding the data model in two layers simultaneously
Any rename or restructuring of SemanticFieldExplorer's result classes requires changes in the API layer
findings.suggestion: Expose SemanticFieldExplorer results through stable accessor methods or move the result types to a shared results package so the API layer does not depend on inner-class details of SemanticFieldExplorer.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 1000+ line monolith that handles routing, parameter parsing, validation, business orchestration, and JSON serialization for 10+ endpoints
evidence: start() method is 230+ lines registering all endpoints inline with full lambda bodies
Handler methods such as handleSemanticFieldExplore (130+ lines) combine parameter parsing, validation, executor calls, and full JSON response construction
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti duplicates the parameter parsing, seed validation, and edge-building pattern from handleSemanticFieldExplore
findings.suggestion: Break the server into focused handler classes (one per endpoint group) that receive pre-wired dependencies. Extract a separate ResponseBuilder or serialize the explorer result classes directly. The start() method should only contain route registration.

findings.summary: The query package subsumes executor infrastructure, result DTOs, snippet parsing utilities, semantic field exploration logic, and POS group constants — too many concerns for a single package
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor: query execution infrastructure
BlackLabSnippetParser: XML/text parsing utility
SemanticFieldExplorer: multi-step exploration domain logic (700+ lines with its own inner result class hierarchy)
QueryResults: shared result DTOs
PosGroup: domain constants
findings.suggestion: Split the query package: keep query execution infrastructure in query/, move SemanticFieldExplorer + its result types to exploration/ or semantic/, move BlackLabSnippetParser to query/internal or parsing/, and move PosGroup to a shared constants package.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer serves as both a service (exploration/comparison logic) and a result data container (6 static inner result classes), blurring its responsibility
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer contains: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge — all static inner classes
The service methods exploreByPattern() and compare() are in the same file as 6 result/DTO types totalling ~300 lines of inner class definitions
API layer must import individual inner classes: import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, etc.
findings.suggestion: Extract the result types (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult, etc.) to their own top-level classes or a dedicated results sub-package. SemanticFieldExplorer should only contain the service logic.

findings.summary: QueryExecutor declares executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as default methods that fall back to lower-level calls, but there is only one real implementation, making the defaults unused scaffolding
evidence: QueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery() default: return executeQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults) — comment says 'for backward compatibility'
QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern() default: return findCollocations(lemma, bcqlPattern, ...) — comment says 'ignores position hints'
BlackLabQueryExecutor overrides both, so the defaults are never exercised in the production code path
getExecutorType() default returns getClass().getSimpleName() — useful pattern, but the other two defaults only exist to ease a non-existent alternate implementation
findings.suggestion: If only one implementation exists, declare executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as abstract interface methods (no defaults) to make the contract explicit. Remove the backward-compatibility comment which implies an obsolete migration that is already complete.

findings.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes seven near-identical methods for extracting lemmas from XML or text at a given position, creating a fragmented API with overlapping responsibilities
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch(String matchText) — finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromSnippet(String snippet) — also finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(String xmlSnippet, int position) — finds lemma at 1-based position in XML
extractCollocateFromMatch(String matchOnly, int labelPos) — same as above, different name
extractTokenFromSnippet(String snippet, int position) — same pattern, falls back to last lemma
extractCollocateFromMatchText(String matchText, int position) — whitespace-separated variant
extractCollocateFromPlainText(String text, int position) — another whitespace-separated variant
extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateFromSnippet do exactly the same thing (iterate LEMMA_ATTR, return last)
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to two clear methods: extractLemmaAtPosition(String xml, int position) for XML and extractTokenAtPosition(String text, int position) for plain text. Remove duplicates and document the contract (0 = last, n = nth).

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer instantiates SemanticFieldExplorer in four separate handler methods, creating a new wrapper object per request that adds no state beyond the already-held executor reference
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close();
handleSemanticField: same pattern
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern
SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) constructor stores only the executor reference — no per-instance state is added beyond what the server already holds
findings.suggestion: Hold a single SemanticFieldExplorer as a field of WordSketchApiServer (initialized in the constructor), or make the exploration methods static taking a QueryExecutor parameter. Either eliminates the per-request create/close cycle.

findings.summary: pom.xml declares com.alibaba:fastjson at version 2.0.25, but all code imports from com.alibaba.fastjson2.* — the correct artifact for that package is com.alibaba:fastjson2
evidence: pom.xml line 45: <artifactId>fastjson</artifactId> <version>2.0.25</version>
HttpApiUtils.java line 3: import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON;
WordSketchApiServer.java line 1-3: import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON; import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSONArray; import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSONObject;
The com.alibaba.fastjson2.* namespace belongs to artifact com.alibaba:fastjson2, not com.alibaba:fastjson
findings.suggestion: Change pom.xml to declare <artifactId>fastjson2</artifactId> with the same version to make the dependency explicit. The current setup works only if fastjson2 classes are present via a transitive dependency from BlackLab or another library, which is fragile.

findings.summary: pom.xml defines a lucene.version property (8.11.1) that is never used as a direct dependency, creating a false impression of the Lucene version in use
evidence: pom.xml line 18: <lucene.version>8.11.1</lucene.version>
No dependency in the pom.xml references ${lucene.version}
Lucene is only brought in transitively through blacklab-engine; BlackLab 4.0.0 bundles a different Lucene version than 8.11.1
findings.suggestion: Remove the unused lucene.version property. If it was intended as documentation, add a comment explaining which Lucene version BlackLab 4.0 pulls in transitively.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer.start() is a 230+ line method registering 10 endpoint handlers inline with full request handling logic in each lambda body
evidence: start() registers /health, /api/sketch/, /api/relations, /api/relations/dep, /api/semantic-field/explore, /api/semantic-field/explore-multi, /api/semantic-field, /api/semantic-field/examples, /api/concordance/examples, /api/visual/radial, /api/bcql — all inline
Each handler lambda calls a private handleXxx method, but OPTIONS/method guard boilerplate is duplicated in every lambda body rather than being extracted
findings.suggestion: Extract the repeated OPTIONS/method-guard pattern into HttpApiUtils or a helper. Optionally use a dispatcher map or register individual endpoint methods directly to reduce start() to route registration only.

findings.summary: The pattern of building a JSONObject per collocate (lemma, frequency, logDice, pos) and appending to a JSONArray is duplicated verbatim across four handler methods
evidence: handleRelationQuery lines 361-369: for loop building JSONObject word with lemma/frequency/logDice/pos
handleDependencyRelationQuery lines 462-470: identical loop
handleFullSketch lines 318-327: identical loop inside an outer relation loop
handleFullDependencySketch lines 414-423: identical loop inside an outer relation loop
findings.suggestion: Extract a private static JSONObject toCollocateJson(WordSketchResult r) helper and call it from all four sites. This reduces 40+ duplicated lines to 4 calls.

findings.summary: SimpleTagger's constructor contains ~80 hard-coded lexicon.put() calls for word-to-tag mappings, embedding test corpus data as production code
evidence: SimpleTagger() constructor lines 113-210 contain explicit put() calls for words like house, dog, cat, park, bed, hill, man, garden, etc.
Comments like '// More adjectives from test corpus' and '// Common nouns' confirm this is test data baked into production code
The loadLexicon(String file) method already exists to load from a file, but the constructor pre-populates with hard-coded entries instead of delegating to it
findings.suggestion: Move the hard-coded entries to a classpath resource file (e.g., /tagger/default_lexicon.txt). Load it in the constructor with loadLexicon(). This keeps the code clean and makes the lexicon maintainable without recompilation.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer implements AutoCloseable but is created and closed manually in handler methods, so exceptions between creation and close() leak resources
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close(); — no try-with-resources
handleSemanticField: same pattern
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern
If the business logic between creation and close() throws, close() is never called
findings.suggestion: Wrap all three instantiations in try (SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)) { ... } to guarantee close() is always called. Alternatively, as noted elsewhere, make the explorer a shared field.

findings.summary: RadialPlot.toSVG() mixes string concatenation with StringBuilder.append and String.format in an inconsistent style within the same method
evidence: Line 163: svg.append("<svg ... width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ..." — string concatenation inside append
Line 177: svg.append("  <rect width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ...) — same pattern
Line 168: svg.append(String.format("    .guide-circle { ...stroke-width: %s; }\n", fmt(...))) — uses String.format
Line 163 and 177 could use String.format or a template for consistency with adjacent lines
findings.suggestion: Pick one string-building style for the SVG template lines. Using String.format consistently (already used for most lines) is clearest. Alternatively, use a simple template with placeholders and replace all at once.
